value.value: Any liquid intended for human consumption.
A substance used in making a drink.
The primary ingredient that defines the character of the drink.
Fruit, vegetable, or other produce from which juice is extracted.
Milk consumed as a beverage on its own.
Mixed drink made by combining two or more drinks or ingredients. May or may not contain alcohol.
Fermented beverage made from apple or pear juice.
Fermented tea drink. May contain trace amounts of alcohol but generally classified as non-alcoholic.
Sweetened spirit flavored with fruit, cream, herbs, or spices.
Infusion of herbs, flowers, or fruit. Not derived from Camellia sinensis (tea plant).
Neutral spirit distilled from grain, potatoes, grapes, or other starch/sugar sources.
Savory liquid made by simmering meat, bones, or vegetables in water.
Beverage containing stimulants such as caffeine, taurine, and B vitamins.
Blended drink typically made from fruit, vegetables, or both.
Drink containing protein powder, often mixed with milk or water.
Any drink containing a dairy ingredient. Defined class: automatically classifies drinks with dairy.
